questions.q: What will be the volume strength of 100 ml of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ required to react completely with 61 ml of $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ in acidic medium? (Given that 61 ml of $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ reacts completely with 5 ml of 1 M of $\mathrm{K}_4\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]$ where it converts into $\mathrm{K}^{+}, \mathrm{Fe}^{3+}, \mathrm{CO}_3^{2-}$ and $\left.\mathrm{NO}_3^{-}\right)$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: 0.10 g of a sample containing $\mathrm{CuCO}_3$ and some inert impurity was dissolved in dilute sulphuric acid and volume made up to 50 ml . This solution was added into 50 ml of 0.04 M KI solution where copper precipitates as $\mathrm{Cul}^{-1} \mathrm{I}^{-}$is oxidised into $\mathrm{I}_3^{-}$. A 10 ml portion of this solution is taken for analysis filtered and made up free $I_3^{-}$and then treated with excess of acidic permanganate solution. Liberated iodine required 20 ml of 2.5 mM sodium thiosulphate solution to reach the end point. Determine the wt. percentage of $\mathrm{CuCo}_3$ in the original sample.
questions.solution:
m moles of $\mathrm{KI}=50 \times 0.04=2$
m.eq. of $\mathrm{Na}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$ reacted with 10 ml solution $=20 \times 2.5 \times 10^{-3}$
$=50 \times 10^{-3}$
m.eq. of I' left unreacted in 100 ml solution $=50 \times 10^{-3} \times \frac{100}{10}=0.5$
m . moles of I reacted with $\mathrm{CuCO}_3=2-0.5=1.5$
$\because 2 \mathrm{Cu}^{++}+5 \mathrm{I}^{-} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{CuI}+\mathrm{I}_3^{-}$
m.moles of $\mathrm{Cu}^{++}=\frac{2}{5} \times 1.5=0.6$
wt. of $\mathrm{CuCO}_3=0.6 \times 10^{-3} \times 123.5$
$=0.0741$
wt. of $\mathrm{CuCO}_3=\frac{0.0741}{0.1} \times 100=74.1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: How many ml of $0.05 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{KMnO}_4$ (acid) are required to oxidize 2.0 gram of $\mathrm{FeSO}_4$ in dilute solution?
questions.solution:
The redox changes are
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{Mn}^{7+}+5 \mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Mn}^{2+} \\ & \mathrm{Fe}^{2+} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Fe}^{3+}+1 \mathrm{e}^{-}\end{aligned}$
Equivalent weight of $\mathrm{FeSO}_4=\frac{152}{1}$ Milli equivalent of $\mathrm{KMnO}_4=$ Meq. Of $\mathrm{FeSO}_4$
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore 0.05 \times 5 \times \mathrm{V}=\frac{2}{152 / 1} \times 1000 \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{V}=52.63 \mathrm{ml}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
